purpose: Single source of truth for recurring project questions on this {{STACK_LABEL}} workspace. Agents MUST consult this registry before answering 'how do we do X in this project'. Adding/changing entries requires explicit user approval — this protects determinism.

decisions.question: What is the project stack?
decisions.answer: {{STACK_LABEL}} on Python {{STACK_LANGUAGE_VERSION}} (project venv at `{{PYTHON_BIN}}`). Workspace root: `{{WORKSPACE_ROOT}}`.

decisions.question: Which Python interpreter do we run for this project?
decisions.answer: Always use the project venv: `{{PYTHON_BIN}}`. Never a system-PATH `python`. The venv path is exported as `{{ENV_PREFIX}}_PYTHON_BIN` in `.codex/mcp.local.env`.

decisions.question: Where does the project code live?
How do we verify changes in this project?
decisions.answer: Run the project's test suite (`pytest`, `unittest`, framework runner) on the smallest unit affected. For real-data probes use the `codebase` MCP read-only tools first; only escalate to running code when search is insufficient.

decisions.question: Which MCP do we use for which question?
decisions.answer: Codebase discovery, file search, model graph → `codebase` MCP. Other servers are not installed in this preset. Never broad-read files before asking the codebase MCP first.

decisions.question: Which language do we reply in?
decisions.answer: Reply in {{RESPONSE_LANGUAGE}} unless the user explicitly asks otherwise. Code identifiers and comments stay in English so the codebase is searchable.

decisions.question: Should rules and skills hard-code specific names?
decisions.answer: No. Rules and skills must be generic and operate on any code in the workspace. Discover modules/files at runtime via the `codebase` MCP — never embed names into invariant rules.

decisions.question: How do we keep answers consistent across conversations?
decisions.answer: Before answering recurring questions, call `lookup_canonical_decision` via the `codebase` MCP. If a match exists, return the registered answer verbatim and cite `registry_path` + `version`. If no match exists, propose adding a new entry instead of inventing a one-shot answer.

decisions.question: How do we run a deep review/audit so the count is consistent across sessions?
decisions.answer: Follow `.cursor/rules/audit-methodology.mdc`. Step 0: read `.codex/audit_findings_locked.md` FIRST and cite the recorded count verbatim. Step 1: cover all audit dimensions in ONE pass. Step 2: uniform severity (BLOCKER / MEDIUM / LOW) with PROOF lines. Drip-feed across sessions is forbidden.

decisions.question: Where do real credentials live?
decisions.answer: Real credentials live ONLY in `.codex/mcp.local.env` (gitignored). Committed files must contain placeholders or env-var names — never real passwords or hostnames. Env-var prefix for this project: `{{ENV_PREFIX}}`.

decisions.question: How are durable rules enforced on edits?
decisions.answer: `.claude/hooks/invariant_guard.py` runs PreToolUse on Edit/Write/MultiEdit. It reads `.agent-toolkit/invariants.json` and DENIES any edit that strips a `must_keep_regex` pattern with severity `blocker`. Bypass once by adding `bypass-invariant: <id>` to the next user prompt. Permanent changes go through `/adr-add` → `/inv-add`.
